proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5 – Semantic Correctness (the “no contracts” message is guaranteed when there are none)
; ------------------------------------------------------------
; Model the relevant state:
;   contracts_len – number of contracts returned by ContractStore.getAll()
;   logged       – 1 if the “No contracts found …” line is executed, 0 otherwise
(declare-const contracts_len Int)
(declare-const logged Int)          ; 0 = not logged, 1 = logged

; ------------------------------------------------------------------
; Code guarantees (pre‑condition for reaching the signal):
;   The signal is emitted only when contracts_len == 0
(assert (= contracts_len 0))

; ------------------------------------------------------------------
; Transition imposed by the implementation:
;   If there are no contracts then the log statement is executed,
;   otherwise it is not.
;   (=> condition (logged == 1))
;   (=> not‑condition (logged == 0))
(assert (=> (= contracts_len 0) (= logged 1)))
(assert (=> (not (= contracts_len 0)) (= logged 0)))

; ------------------------------------------------------------------
; Negation of the desired property (i.e. “contracts_len == 0 but the
; message is NOT printed”).  Proving unsat shows the property holds.
(assert (= contracts_len 0))   ; condition holds
(assert (= logged 0))          ; but the log did NOT happen

; ------------------------------------------------------------
; If Z3 returns unsat, the property is proven: the program cannot
; reach a state where contracts_len is zero and the message is absent.
(check-sat)
